back_translation: 'At this too my heart pounds and nearly leaps from its place.
Listen closely to the thunder of His voice and the rumbling that comes from His mouth.
He unleashes His lightning across the whole sky and sends it to the ends of the earth.
After it comes a roaring sound; He thunders with His majestic voice and does not hold back the lightning when His voice resounds.
God thunders wondrously with His voice; He does great things beyond our understanding.
For He says to the snow, 'Fall on the earth,' and to the rain, 'Pour down heavily.'
He stops the work of every man's hand, so that all may know His work.
The wild animals go into their hiding places and stay in their dens.
The storm comes from its chamber, and the cold from the driving north winds.
By the breath of God the ice is formed, and the broad waters freeze solid.
He loads the clouds with moisture and scatters His lightning through them.
The clouds whirl about at His guidance, doing all that He commands over the face of the earth.
Whether for punishment, or for His land, or to show steadfast love, He makes these things happen.
Listen to this, O Job; stand still and consider the wonders of God.
Do you know how God commands the clouds and makes the lightning flash?
Do you understand how the clouds float, the wonders of Him who is perfect in knowledge?
You whose clothes are hot when the land lies still under the south wind,
can you spread out the skies as He does, strong as a mirror of bronze?
Teach us what we should say to Him; we cannot arrange our case because of our darkness.
Should He be told that I wish to speak? Would any man ask to be swallowed up?
Now no one can look at the sun when it shines brightly in the skies after the wind has swept them clean.
He comes from the north in golden splendor; awesome majesty surrounds God.
The Almighty is beyond our reach; He is exalted in power, and in His justice and great righteousness He does not oppress.
Therefore men fear Him, for He shows no regard for any who think themselves wise.'
